task: I’m drafting a short reply to a seller who just revised the terms after we were already moving toward a deal. I want to keep the message brief and professional while making the next step clear.

turns.agent_response:
Use this:

> We’re not able to accept the revised terms. Please send the final version of the agreement, or confirm that we should proceed under the prior terms.

If you want it firmer:

> We’re not accepting the revised terms. Please confirm whether you’d like to proceed under the prior terms or end discussions.

user_simulator.text:
Close. But I’d tighten it a bit. "We’re not able to accept" is softer than I’d use if they already moved the goalposts.

Something like:

We are not accepting the revised terms. Please confirm whether you want to proceed under the prior terms or end discussions.

If you want it a little less abrupt:

We can’t move forward on the revised terms. Please confirm whether you’d like to proceed under the prior terms or end discussions.

I’d use the first one if you want leverage.

turns.agent_response:
Use this:

> We are not accepting the revised terms. Please confirm whether you want to proceed under the prior terms or end discussions.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clean and firm.
